description_long: This is a hand-painted cylindrical barrel or bucket with a slightly tapered shape and an open top. The exterior is decorated with colorful folk art, including a swan on a pond, a yellow house, a windmill, and floral motifs, all bordered by dotted lines. The rim appears rough and unglazed, while the body has a matte, textured finish, suggesting it is made of ceramic or clay. The interior is dark and unadorned, indicating it is hollow and can serve as a receptacle. No handles or lids are visible, and there are no structural reinforcements or metal bands. The object is likely intended for decorative or light utility use in an outdoor or garden setting.
description: A hand-painted, open-topped ceramic barrel or bucket with folk art scenes and floral motifs, featuring a rough, matte finish and no visible handles or lid.
description_view_0: The open mouth of the barrel or bucket is visible, showing the dark, hollow interior and the rough, unglazed rim.
description_view_1: The side displays painted scenes of a windmill, a yellow house, and floral decorations bordered by dotted lines.
description_view_2: The base of the barrel or bucket is visible, showing a faceted, polygonal bottom with green paint and no additional features.
description_view_3: A painted swan on a pond and more floral motifs are visible, with the matte, textured surface clearly shown.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: hand-painted folk art scenes and floral motifs
